testPlan: Verify the file set lands both order endpoints in a single route file: app/api/orders/route.ts (exports GET + POST).
Verify GET /api/orders defaults to ?status=active when the parameter is missing.
Verify GET /api/orders returns 400 'status must be active or closed' on invalid status (e.g. ?status=foo, ?status=delivered).
Verify GET /api/orders returns 401 without a session cookie.
Verify GET /api/orders maps each row to the documented projection: id, status, placedAt (ISO string), expectedDelivery (ISO string or null), itemCount, totalPaise, distributor: { id, name }.
Verify GET /api/orders 'closed' returns only delivered/cancelled rows; 'active' returns only placed/acknowledged/out_for_delivery rows.
Verify POST /api/orders returns 409 'cart is empty' when nothing to fan out.
Verify POST /api/orders fans out one order per distinct distributor and returns { orderIds, orderCount } per the CTO contract.
Verify POST /api/orders clears the cart on success (downstream invariant; relies on createOrdersFromCart).
Search files[].content for forbidden placeholder strings.

results.test: Single route file exports GET + POST.
results.details: app/api/orders/route.ts declares `export async function GET` and `export async function POST`. Next 14 multi-method route convention.
raw = (searchParams.get('status') ?? 'active').toLowerCase(). When the param is missing, falls into the active branch and listOrders is called with group='active'. Confirmed by tracing GET head.

if (raw !== 'active' && raw !== 'closed') -> 400 'status must be active or closed'. ?status=delivered (a valid OrderStatus but not a valid GROUP) is correctly rejected because the parameter is the *group*, not the status itself.
if (!session) -> 401 'unauthenticated'. Same defence-in-depth pattern as the other authenticated reads.

results.test: GET projection matches the CTO contract.
results.details: Each row mapped to { id, status, placedAt: o.placedAt.toISOString(), expectedDelivery: o.expectedDelivery ? toISOString : null, itemCount, totalPaise, distributor: { id, name } | { id, name: 'Unknown distributor' } }. ISO conversion explicit because Date does not auto-serialise as ISO via NextResponse.json's default.

listOrders is the source of truth for filtering (verified in t-data-1 audit). The route only translates the group string and trusts the helper. Co-located vitest verifies that for both 'active' and 'closed' every returned row has a status from the corresponding ACTIVE/CLOSED set.
createOrdersFromCart returns []. if (created.length === 0) -> 409 'cart is empty'. Cart UI can show an empty-state message rather than misreading a 200 as success.

On non-empty cart: createOrdersFromCart returns Array<{ id, distributorId }>. Response: { orderIds: created.map(c => c.id), orderCount: created.length }. Co-located vitest verifies a 2-distributor cart produces orderCount: 2.

results.test: POST clears the cart on success.
results.details: createOrdersFromCart calls clearCart(retailerId) at the end of its synchronous block (verified in t-data-1 audit). The route does not need to clear it again. Atomicity is guaranteed by the synchronous JS event loop in the in-memory implementation.
`export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` declared after imports. Required because both GET and POST call cookies() via getSession().
